title: Discrete Mathematics - Set Theory and Algebra - Countable Uncountable Set

questions.question:
Consider the following sets.

  1. The set of all natural numbers \( \mathbb{N} \).
  2. The set of all rational numbers \( \mathbb{Q} \).
  3. The set of all functions from \( \mathbb{N} \) to \( \{1,2,3,4,5\} \).
  4. The set of all functions from \( \{1,2,3,4,5\} \) to \( \mathbb{N} \).

Which of the above sets are countable?



Set Theory & Algebra: TIFR CSE 2025 | Part B | Question: 3
